changes_summary: Addressed FLAG-001 by narrowing every grep verification for `_can_user_message_bot` to `src/features/admin_chat/` plus `tests/` (instead of the full `src/` tree) and explicitly documenting that the same-name method in `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py` is a live, unrelated sibling that must remain untouched. Added a Sibling-file caveat in the Overview, refined Step 1's audit wording, qualified Step 7's grep scope, Step 10's cross-directory grep expectation, Step 11's hasattr assertion comment, Step 12's final grep instructions, and the corresponding success criterion. No scope, test-set, or implementation changes beyond what the flag required.
questions: The brief allows either `claude-haiku-4-5` or the currently configured `self._classifier_model` (today `claude-opus-4-6`). I'm keeping the existing model string since swapping models is extra surface area; confirm that's acceptable, or tell me to flip it to Haiku in Step 3.

The brief says the classifier must be stateless and see only the single recipient reply and the bot's prompt. I'm baking the bot's prompt into a static `_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM_PROMPT` constant rather than fetching the exact prior bot message from the channel. OK, or do you want the real prior bot message pulled from the thread?

`test_approved_member_no_longer_routed_to_agent` at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:697` is fully redundant with the fall-through test at line 654 once `_can_user_message_bot` is gone. I'm planning to delete it outright. Confirm that's preferred over rewriting it.

`tools.py` currently exposes `ALL_TOOL_NAMES` as a module-level constant derived from the MEMBER/ADMIN union. If nothing outside the assertions references it, I'll delete it too; otherwise I'll keep a direct `ALL_TOOL_NAMES = {t['name'] for t in TOOLS}` form. A grep during Step 9 will decide. Flag if you want it kept unconditionally.

success_criteria.criterion: `admin_chat_cog.py` contains an async instance method `_classify_confirmation` that calls `self._classifier_client.messages.create` with `tools=[...]` and `tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "classify_payment_reply"}` when `self._classifier_client` is not None.
When `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is unset (so `self._classifier_client is None`), `_classify_confirmation` returns the result of the keyword classifier without making any network calls.

When `self._classifier_client.messages.create` raises an exception, `_classify_confirmation` catches it, logs a warning, and returns the keyword classifier result — fail-closed to keyword, not to auto-positive.

The LLM `confirmed` / `not_received` / `unclear` outputs map to `positive` / `negative` / `ambiguous` respectively, and an `unclear` reply with a non-empty `reply` field propagates that string to `_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous` via the new `clarification_reply` kwarg.

`_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous` posts a recipient-directed clarification message (default template OR the LLM-provided `reply`) to the channel on the first ambiguous reply (post-increment count `< 2`), and the existing 2-strike escalation to `_handle_test_receipt_negative` still fires on the second ambiguous reply.

`_handle_test_receipt_negative` sends two channel messages: one recipient-directed (`Hey <@recipient> — I've flagged this for admin review. Please wait for <@admin> to follow up.`) and the existing admin-tagged `detail` message. The admin DM via `_notify_intent_admin` is still sent.

`MEMBER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH`, `MEMBER_TOOLS`, `ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS`, `get_tools_for_role`, and the admin_chat-local `_can_user_message_bot` and `_is_directed_at_bot` methods are removed from the source tree. Grep verification: `_is_directed_at_bot` and the other symbols return zero hits across `src/` and `tests/`; `_can_user_message_bot` returns zero hits across `src/features/admin_chat/` and `tests/` (the unrelated `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py` retains its own implementation and is out of scope).

`AdminChatAgent.chat()` no longer accepts an `is_admin` parameter; `agent.py` uses `SYSTEM_PROMPT` and `TOOLS` unconditionally, and the cog call site at `admin_chat_cog.py:1144-1151` is updated accordingly.

`tests/test_admin_payments.py` includes new tests covering: LLM happy path (confirmed/not_received/unclear+reply), keyword fallback when API key missing, keyword fallback when API raises, first-ambiguous clarification (default + LLM-provided reply), negative-path recipient-facing message, and dead-code removal.

`tests/test_social_route_tools.py` is updated so that `test_route_tools_are_admin_only` and `test_admin_tool_registration_and_identity_injection_sets` no longer reference `get_tools_for_role` or `ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS`, and both pass against the post-cleanup `TOOLS` list.

`pytest -x -q` passes end-to-end (all previously passing tests still pass; new tests pass; the single intentionally deleted `test_approved_member_no_longer_routed_to_agent` is removed rather than skipped).

No files outside `src/features/admin_chat/` and `tests/` are modified. Specifically, no changes to `src/features/admin/admin_cog.py`, `src/features/grants/solana_client.py`, `src/features/payments/*`, `src/common/db_handler.py`, `producer_flows.py`, the state-machine transitions themselves, the orphan sweep, `AdminApprovalView`, or the identity-routing query/batch tools.

Fail-closed posture is preserved: classifier exceptions, missing API key, malformed tool_use blocks, and unknown `classification` values all route to the keyword classifier (not to an auto-confirm path).

The new `_classify_confirmation` is stateless — no channel history, no cross-call memory; the only inputs passed to the Anthropic API are the static `_CLASSIFIER_SYSTEM_PROMPT` and a single user message containing the raw recipient reply.
Classifier prompt is strict about 'ok'/'thanks' alone not counting as confirmed and 'wait let me check' counting as unclear, per the brief.
`admin_chat_cog.py` stays roughly the same size after cleanup — net line count delta is small (new classifier method + small handler edits − ~60 lines of dead methods), not a sprawling rewrite.
Recipient-directed messages use the existing `<@id>` mention convention consistent with the rest of the cog and avoid introducing a new mention-formatting helper.

Manual smoke test: admin triggers a real test payment, recipient replies 'ok thanks', bot classifies as unclear and posts the LLM-provided clarification; recipient then replies 'confirmed', bot moves to admin approval. Out-of-scope for automated validation.

Manual smoke test: classifier exception path (e.g. temporarily revoke API key in staging) still routes an ambiguous-looking reply through keyword fallback and escalates correctly. Out-of-scope for automated validation.

assumptions: The `requester_id` plumbing inside `tools.py` (lines 3737-3767) and the `_ADMIN_IDENTITY_INJECTED_TOOLS` injection in `agent.py` are part of the identity-routing megaplan and stay untouched, even though `requester_id` will always be `None` post-cleanup.

The classifier's `reply` field is used only by the ambiguous path — the negative path uses its own hardcoded recipient notice regardless of classifier output, consistent with the brief's description of fix #3.

Keyword classifier tests at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:587-607` can be re-pointed at the renamed `_classify_confirmation_keyword` helper without losing coverage, because the keyword logic is byte-for-byte identical.
`_classifier_model` stays at the current string; the brief explicitly permits using the existing value.

The autouse `admin_chat_env` fixture that deletes `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is the intended default — new LLM-path tests will override it with `monkeypatch.setenv` and stub `cog._classifier_client` directly, so no real Anthropic client is ever constructed during tests.

The baseline of 189 tests is approximate; the success criterion is 'no existing test regresses', not 'exactly 189 pass after the change', since this plan adds ~7 new tests and deletes one redundant test.

`_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous` receiving the LLM's clarification reply as a kwarg is a strict addition — the recursive call from inside itself on the `>= 2` branch is replaced by a direct call to `_handle_test_receipt_negative`, and clarification text is never forwarded into the negative path.

Messages sent via the newly added `message.channel.send` calls do not need Discord mention-ping suppression beyond what the existing surrounding code already does; the existing handler freely uses raw `<@id>` mentions, so the new messages follow the same convention.

`src/features/admin/admin_cog.py` is a separate feature (the admin DM-routing cog) that coincidentally defines a same-named `_can_user_message_bot` helper for its own purposes. It is not part of admin_chat, not referenced by admin_chat, and explicitly out of scope for this cleanup — grep targets are scoped accordingly.
